resources.string: Already have an account? Sign In

string-array.name: activity_levels
string-array.item: Sedentary
Lightly Active
Moderately Active
Very Active

string-array.name: fitness_goals
string-array.item: Lose Weight
Maintain Weight
Gain Weight

string-array.name: health_tips
string-array.item: Stay hydrated! Drink at least 8 glasses of water today.
Aim for 30 minutes of moderate exercise most days of the week.
Fill half your plate with fruits and vegetables.
Get at least 7-8 hours of quality sleep per night.
Practice mindfulness or meditation for a few minutes each day.
Incorporate strength training into your routine at least twice a week.
Limit processed foods and sugary drinks.
Take short breaks to stretch and move around if you sit for long periods.
Eat a balanced breakfast to kickstart your metabolism.
Listen to your body and rest when you need to.

string-array.name: workout_tips
string-array.item: Don\'t forget to warm up for 5-10 minutes before every workout.
Focus on form over weight to prevent injuries and get better results.
Consistency is key! Stick to your training plan even on busy days.
Stay hydrated during your training session.
Breath control: exhale during exertion, inhale during release.
Rest is just as important as training—allow 48 hours for muscle groups to recover.
Track your progress: log your sets, reps, and weights.
Fuel your body with a small snack 30-60 minutes before training.
Stretching after a workout helps reduce muscle soreness.
Listen to your body—stop if you feel sharp pain.

string-array.name: cooking_tips
string-array.item: Swap heavy cream for Greek yogurt in sauces for a protein boost and less fat.
Use spices and herbs like cumin, turmeric, or fresh cilantro instead of salt for flavor.
Try air-frying or baking instead of deep-frying to significantly reduce oil intake.
Substitute white rice with cauliflower rice or brown rice for more fiber.
Sauté vegetables using water or low-sodium broth instead of butter or oil.
Always wash your lentils and grains thoroughly to remove excess starch.
Marinate lean proteins like chicken or tofu for at least 30 minutes to lock in moisture without extra fat.
Add plenty of leafy greens like spinach or kale to your daals and curries.
Use non-stick cookware to minimize the amount of oil needed for cooking.
Prepare your meals in batches to avoid the temptation of unhealthy takeout.

resources.string: Automatically download attachments for incoming emails
Only download attachments when manually requested
